metadata.summary: Hard-code compaction schema to preserve file paths, function names, error messages, decisions, URLs, IDs. Drop reasoning and tool output. Output is a reference manifest for resume.

content.title: Compaction Templates That Preserve References
content.summary: When a long-running agent must compact its conversation to free context, the compaction prompt must hard-code which fields survive and which are dropped. Always preserve: file paths, function names, error messages, decisions, URLs, IDs. Always drop: intermediate reasoning chains and verbatim tool output. The compacted summary is itself a manifest of references — not a narrative — so the agent can re-fetch any dropped detail by path or ID instead of re-reasoning from scratch.

why.p: Free-form compaction prompts ("summarize the conversation so far") let the model invent the schema each time. The result drifts: sometimes the file paths survive, sometimes the reasoning does, sometimes a critical error message gets paraphrased into uselessness. Anthropic's context-engineering cookbook and lethain's "Building an internal agent" both report that the failure mode of post-compaction agents is loss of pointers, not loss of prose: the model has the gist but cannot find the file it was about to edit. A schema'd compaction protects exactly the data the agent needs to resume.

list.item: Any agent loop expected to exceed approximately 30 turns or its context window — compaction will trigger.
Multi-session SDD work where the next session reads the compacted output and resumes.
Pipelines where compaction is automated by middleware (opencode, Claude Code auto-compaction).
Whenever the agent depends on file paths, error texts, or external IDs to make progress.
Stateless single-turn agents — there is no later step that needs the references.
Conversations that compact only the user-facing answer for display, not for the agent's own continuation.
Agents whose only "state" is a closed-form result (a number, a yes/no) — references add noise.

section.name: keep-drop-rule
section.p: Every item the agent will need to re-fetch context or resume action is a reference. References must survive verbatim — not paraphrased.

rule.statement: Compacted output MUST preserve verbatim: absolute or workspace-relative file paths, fully-qualified function/class names, error message strings, URLs, IDs (PR numbers, ticket IDs, execution IDs), explicit decisions ("chose X over Y because Z").
rule.rationale: These are pointers the agent and its downstream tools dereference. Paraphrasing breaks the dereference (a wrong path is worse than no path).

example.code: files_touched: - src/auth/login.py:42 - src/auth/jwt.py:88-110 errors_open: - "test_login_invalid_pw fails at AssertionError: expected 401 got 200" decisions: - "RS256 over HS256 (ADR-007)"
example.note: Every entry survives a regex search. The next session can grep, open, edit.

rule.statement: Compacted output MUST drop: intermediate reasoning chains, tool-call argument repetition, verbatim tool output bodies, and conversational filler ("let me think", "ok", "as we discussed").
rule.rationale: Reasoning is regenerable from inputs; tool output is restorable from disk via path. Keeping them inflates the compacted blob and squeezes the surviving references.

example.code: summary: | We worked on the auth module for a while. I thought about JWT algorithms and decided RS256 was probably better. Then we ran the tests and one of them failed. I'll look at it next time.
example.note: Prose; no path, no error string, no ID. Worthless on resume.

antipattern.description: Compacting via "summarize the conversation in 200 words" with no schema.
antipattern.reason: The model invents the schema each call; references survive only by accident.

section.name: schema-compaction-template
section.p: The compacted state is a YAML document with a fixed set of top-level keys. The compaction prompt instructs the model to fill each key from the conversation; missing data becomes an empty list, never a paragraph.

rule.statement: The compacted state MUST be valid YAML with these top-level keys: goal, files_touched (list), decisions (list), errors_open (list), refs (list of {kind, id}), next_action (one sentence). NO free-text "summary" field.
rule.rationale: Fixed keys make the output machine-checkable; missing one key is a hard failure the orchestrator catches before the agent acts on bad state.

example.title: Reference compacted state
example.code: goal: refactor auth module to use refresh-token rotation files_touched: - src/auth/login.py:42 - src/auth/jwt.py:88-110 decisions: - chose RS256 over HS256 (ADR-007) - rotation interval = 15 min errors_open: - test_login_invalid_pw — AssertionError: expected 401 got 200 refs: - {kind: pr, id: "1842"} - {kind: log, id: ".agent/results/pytest_4882.log"} next_action: implement refresh-token rotation in jwt.py
example.note: Every key fillable from the conversation; resume by grep on goal + reading files_touched.

example.title: Bad compacted state
example.code: summary: | Worked on auth, made some progress, will continue next session. status: in-progress
example.note: No keys, no references, no actionable next_action. The next session restarts from zero.

antipattern.description: Letting the LLM add ad-hoc keys ("notes", "thoughts", "todo") that drift between calls.
antipattern.reason: Schema drift means orchestrators can't validate; downstream tooling that expects 'errors_open' breaks when the model writes 'open_errors' instead.

template.purpose: Drop-in compaction system prompt enforcing the references-only schema.
Reference compacted-state file structure with all required keys.

see-also.link: Anthropic Cookbook: context engineering, memory, compaction.
lethain: building an internal agent — context window compaction.
